selection.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.

OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT):
Return ONLY a single-line JSON object, with no extra text, no markdown, no comments.

I also take breaks from social media to avoid getting sucked into the vortex of fake news and sensationalized headlines. response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.

persona.time_onsocialmedia: You spend roughly a few times weekly on social media

response_parsed.opinion: As a fitness trainer and a busy mom, I've learned to navigate the world of social media with a critical eye. With so much misinformation floating around, it's essential to verify the authenticity of the content before sharing or believing it. I make it a point to fact-check information, especially when it comes to health and wellness, which is my area of expertise. I also take breaks from social media to avoid getting sucked into the vortex of fake news and sensationalized headlines. It's easy to get caught up in the drama and negativity, but as someone who's anxious by nature, I know how important it is to maintain a healthy online presence. I've set boundaries for myself, limiting my social media use to a few times a week, and I prioritize face-to-face interactions with friends and family. By being mindful of the content I consume and the time I spend online, I can maintain a sense of control and well-being in a world that often feels overwhelming.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: I've always been a bit of a skeptic, even as a kid. My parents, both with lower secondary education, taught me to question everything and verify information before accepting it as true. As I grew older, I realized the importance of critical thinking, especially in today's digital age. With so much misinformation circulating online, it's up to us to be discerning and not take everything at face value. I've developed this habit over time, even before I became a fitness trainer, when I was just a young adult. I'd often come across questionable health advice online, and I'd fact-check it before sharing it with my friends or family. Now, as a trainer, I take it a step further by verifying information through credible sources before sharing it on social media. I also try to balance my online presence with real-life interactions, which I believe is essential for maintaining a healthy sense of self and relationships. By doing so, I'm able to separate the signal from the noise and avoid getting caught up in the chaos of fake news and sensationalism.
